questions.text: Mount Everest, the world's highest peak, is located on the border of:
questions.explanation: Mount Everest (8,849 m) is located on the border of Nepal and Tibet (China). It was first summited on May 29, 1953 by Edmund Hillary (New Zealand) and Tenzing Norgay (Nepal).

questions.text: Which is the second highest mountain peak in the world?
questions.explanation: K2 (also called Godwin-Austen) at 8,611 m is the world's 2nd highest peak, located in the Karakoram Range in Pakistan-administered Kashmir. Kangchenjunga (8,586 m) is 3rd — the highest peak on Indian territory.

questions.text: The Andes is the world's longest mountain range. It runs along the coast of which continent?
questions.explanation: The Andes (~7,000 km long) runs along the western coast of South America, passing through Venezuela, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, Bolivia, Chile, and Argentina. The highest peak is Aconcagua (6,961 m) in Argentina.

questions.text: What type of mountains are the Himalayas?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas are fold mountains, formed when the Indian tectonic plate collided with the Eurasian plate approximately 50 million years ago. The collision folded up the rocks to form the world's highest mountain range.

questions.text: Which is the highest peak in Africa?
questions.explanation: Mount Kilimanjaro (5,895 m) in Tanzania is the highest peak in Africa. It is a volcanic mountain (stratovolcano), not a fold mountain. Mount Kenya (5,199 m) is 2nd highest in Africa.

questions.text: Which mountain range forms the natural boundary between Europe and Asia?
questions.explanation: The Ural Mountains (running north-south through Russia) form the traditional geographical boundary between Europe and Asia. They are old, heavily eroded mountains with an average height of ~600 m and rich mineral resources.

questions.text: What is the highest peak in North America?
questions.explanation: Denali (formerly Mt. McKinley) at 6,190 m in Alaska, USA is the highest peak in North America. Mt. Logan (5,959 m) is the highest in Canada, and Pico de Orizaba (5,636 m) is the highest in Mexico.

questions.text: A U-shaped valley is formed by:
questions.explanation: U-shaped valleys are formed by glacial erosion. Glaciers carve wider, deeper valleys with flat floors and steep sides (U-shape). Rivers carve V-shaped valleys with a narrow floor. Fjords are drowned U-shaped glacial valleys.

questions.text: The oldest mountain range in India is:
questions.explanation: The Aravalli Range is the oldest fold mountain range in India (and one of the oldest in the world). It runs through Rajasthan, Gujarat, Haryana, and Delhi. Being ancient, it has been heavily eroded and is now relatively low in height.

questions.text: Which plateau is called the 'Roof of the World'?
questions.explanation: The Tibetan Plateau (average ~4,500 m elevation; area ~2.5 million km²) is called the 'Roof of the World.' It is both the highest and largest plateau on Earth, located in Tibet/Southwestern China and India's far north.

questions.text: Mont Blanc, the highest peak in the Alps, is located on the border of:
questions.explanation: Mont Blanc (4,808 m) is located on the border of France and Italy. It is the highest peak in the Alps and in Western Europe (with the broader European definition using Elbrus in Russia as Europe's highest at 5,642 m).

questions.text: Vindhya and Satpura ranges in India are classified as what type of mountains?
questions.explanation: Vindhya and Satpura ranges are block (fault-block) mountains — formed when land between parallel faults was uplifted (horst) or surrounding areas dropped (graben). The Narmada River runs through the rift/graben between them.

questions.text: The highest peak outside Asia is:
questions.explanation: Aconcagua (6,961 m) in the Andes Mountains of Argentina is the highest peak outside Asia. It is the highest in both South America and the Western and Southern Hemispheres.

questions.text: The Grand Canyon was carved by which river?
questions.explanation: The Grand Canyon (446 km long, up to 1.6 km deep) in Arizona, USA was carved over millions of years by the Colorado River. It is one of the world's most famous geomorphological features.

questions.text: Kangchenjunga, the 3rd highest peak in the world, is located on the border of:
questions.explanation: Kangchenjunga (8,586 m) lies on the India-Nepal border in Sikkim/Nepal. It is the highest peak in India or partly in India, and the 3rd highest in the world. K2 (2nd highest) is in Pakistan-administered Kashmir.

questions.text: The Great Rift Valley, a classic example of a rift (graben) landform, is located in:
questions.explanation: The Great Rift Valley runs through East Africa (from the Afar Triangle in Ethiopia south through Kenya, Tanzania, Mozambique). It is a classic rift valley — land sinking between parallel faults due to divergent tectonic movement, also creating the East African Rift lakes.
